title: neogaerrhinum kelloggii
intro: Neogaerrhinum kelloggii, also known as Kellogg's gerrhinum, is a plant species in the family Boraginaceae, native to the western United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including bronchitis, asthma, and coughs, due to its ability to clear mucus and reduce inflammation. Neogaerrhinum kelloggii can be prepared as an infusion or tincture, and has been used in herbal remedies to promote respiratory health and relieve symptoms associated with respiratory conditions.
